secAlertSsl: This connection is not secure. Please avoid entering sensitive information until TLS is activated for data protection.
secAlertConf: Certain settings are vulnerable to attacks. It is recommended to reinforce security protocols to prevent potential breaches.
secAlertSSL: Panel lacks secure connection. Please install TLS certificate for data protection.
secAlertPanelPort: Panel default port is vulnerable. Please configure a random or specific port.
secAlertPanelURI: Panel default URI path is insecure. Please configure a complex URI path.
secAlertSubURI: Subscription default URI path is insecure. Please configure a complex URI path.
secAlertSubJsonURI: Subscription JSON default URI path is insecure. Please configure a complex URI path.

login.title: Welcome
login.loginAgain: Your session has expired, please log in again

toasts.invalidFormData: The input data format is invalid.
toasts.wrongUsernameOrPassword: Invalid username or password or two-factor code.
toasts.successLogin: You have successfully logged into your account.

index.title: Overview
index.xraySwitchClick: Choose the version you want to switch to.
index.xraySwitchClickDesk: Choose carefully, as older versions may not be compatible with current configurations.
index.panelUpdateDesc: This will update 3X-UI itself to the latest release and restart the panel service.
index.devChannelWarning: Dev builds track every commit on main and aren't stable releases — there is no automatic downgrade.
index.xrayErrorPopoverTitle: An error occurred while running Xray
index.xrayMetricsHint: Add a top-level metrics block to the xray config with tag metrics_out and listen 127.0.0.1:11111, then restart xray.
index.xrayObservatoryHint: Add an observatory block to the xray config listing the outbound tags to probe, then restart xray.
index.systemLoadDesc: System load average for the past 1, 5, and 15 minutes
index.xraySwitchVersionDialog: Do you really want to change the Xray version?
index.xraySwitchVersionDialogDesc: This will change the Xray version to #version#.
index.panelUpdateDialog: Do you really want to update the panel?
index.panelUpdateDialogDesc: This will update 3X-UI to #version# and restart the panel service.
index.panelUpdateFailedDesc: The update did not finish successfully. Check the server logs, or run 'x-ui update' from the command line.
index.panelUpdateUnknownDesc: The panel didn't report a result in time. Reload to check the current version, or check the server logs.
index.geofileUpdateDialog: Do you really want to update the geofile?
index.geodataHint: Xray downloads these files on schedule and hot-reloads them without a restart. URLs must be HTTPS. Each file must already exist in the bin folder once before Xray can update it.
index.geodataConfirmContent: This updates the Xray config template and restarts Xray.
index.geodataInvalidUrl: Each file needs an HTTPS URL.
index.geodataInvalidFile: File names must be plain names like geosite_custom.dat (no paths).
index.geodataEmpty: No files configured. Reference files in routing rules as ext:geosite_custom.dat:category.
index.dontRefresh: Installation is in progress, please do not refresh this page
index.exportDatabaseDesc: Click to download a .db file containing a backup of your current database to your device.
index.importDatabaseDesc: Click to select and upload a .db file from your device to restore your database from a backup.
index.importDatabaseSuccess: The database has been successfully imported.
index.importDatabaseError: An error occurred while importing the database.
index.readDatabaseError: An error occurred while reading the database.
index.getDatabaseError: An error occurred while retrieving the database.
index.getConfigError: An error occurred while retrieving the config file.
index.backupPostgresNote: This panel runs on PostgreSQL. Back Up downloads a pg_dump archive (.dump) and Restore loads it back with pg_restore. The server needs the PostgreSQL client tools (pg_dump and pg_restore) installed.
index.exportDatabasePgDesc: Click to download a PostgreSQL dump (.dump) of your current database to your device.
index.importDatabasePgDesc: Click to select and upload a .dump file to restore your PostgreSQL database. This replaces all current data.
index.migrationDownloadDesc: Click to download a portable .dump (SQL text) export of your SQLite database.
index.migrationDownloadPgDesc: Click to download a .db SQLite database built from your PostgreSQL data, ready to run this panel on SQLite.

inbounds.title: Inbounds

fallbacks.title: Fallbacks
fallbacks.help: When a connection on this inbound does not match any client, route it elsewhere. Pick a child inbound below to auto-fill the routing fields (SNI / ALPN / path / xver) from its transport, or leave the picker empty and set Dest directly (e.g. 8080 or 127.0.0.1:8080) to route to an external server such as Nginx. Each child inbound should listen on 127.0.0.1 with security=none.
fallbacks.quickAddedNone: No new eligible inbounds to add
fallbacks.needsTls: Fallbacks become available once Security is set to TLS or Reality on the Security tab (VLESS/Trojan over RAW only).

inbounds.deleteInboundContent: Are you sure you want to delete this inbound?
inbounds.deleteConfirmContent: This removes the inbound and all its clients. This cannot be undone.
inbounds.resetConfirmContent: Resets up/down counters to 0 for this inbound.
inbounds.bulkDeleteConfirmContent: This removes the selected inbounds and all their clients. This cannot be undone.
inbounds.cloneConfirmContent: Creates a copy with a new port and an empty client list.
inbounds.delAllClientsConfirmContent: This removes every client from this inbound and drops their traffic records. The inbound itself is kept. This cannot be undone.
inbounds.attachClientsDesc: Attaches the same {count} clients (same UUID/password and shared traffic) to the selected inbound(s). They stay on this inbound too.
inbounds.attachClientsNoTargets: No other compatible inbounds available to attach to.
inbounds.attachExistingDesc: Attaches existing clients ({count} available) to this inbound — same UUID/password and shared traffic. Clients already on it are skipped.
inbounds.attachExistingNoClients: No clients exist yet. Create clients first, then attach them here.
inbounds.detachClientsDesc: Removes the selected client(s) from this inbound only. Client records themselves are kept (use Delete to remove fully). Source has {count} clients in total.
inbounds.deleteClientContent: Are you sure you want to delete this client?
inbounds.resetTrafficContent: Are you sure you want to reset traffic?
inbounds.monitorDesc: Leave blank to listen on all IPs
inbounds.noRecommendKeepDefault: It is recommended to keep the default
inbounds.clickOnQRcode: Click on QR Code to Copy
inbounds.cloneInboundContent: All settings of this inbound, except Port, Listening IP, and Clients, will be applied to the clone.
inbounds.resetAllTrafficContent: Are you sure you want to reset the traffic of all inbounds?
inbounds.resetInboundClientTrafficContent: Are you sure you want to reset the traffic of this inbound's clients?
inbounds.resetAllClientTrafficContent: Are you sure you want to reset the traffic of all clients?
inbounds.delDepletedClientsContent: Are you sure you want to delete all the depleted clients?
inbounds.emailDesc: Please provide a unique email address.
inbounds.IPLimitDesc: Disables inbound if the count exceeds the set value. (0 = disable)
inbounds.IPLimitlogDesc: The IP history log. (to re-enable the inbound after disabling, clear the log)
inbounds.setDefaultCertEmpty: No certificate is configured for the panel. Set one under Settings first.

advanced.title: Inbound JSON sections
advanced.subtitle: Full inbound JSON and focused editors for settings, sniffing, and streamSettings.
advanced.allHelp: Full inbound object with all fields in one editor.

inbounds.telegramDesc: Please provide Telegram Chat ID. (use '/id' command in the bot) or ({'@'}userinfobot)
inbounds.subscriptionDesc: To find your subscription URL, navigate to the 'Details'. Additionally, you can use the same name for several clients.
inbounds.periodicTrafficResetDesc: Automatically reset traffic counter at specified intervals

toasts.savedNodeOfflineWillSync: Saved locally. A backing node is offline or disabled — the change will sync once it reconnects.
toasts.delDepletedClientsSuccess: All depleted clients have been deleted.
toasts.resetAllClientTrafficSuccess: Traffic for all clients has been reset.
toasts.getNewX25519CertError: Error while obtaining the X25519 certificate.
toasts.scanRealityTargetFeasible: Target is feasible — filled target and SNI.
toasts.scanRealityTargetNotFeasible: Target is reachable but not feasible for REALITY.

form.addAllFallbackTooltip: Add a fallback row for every eligible inbound not yet wired up
form.autoSystemRoutesTooltip: Windows-only. CIDRs added to the system routing table automatically so matching traffic goes through TUN.
form.autoOutboundsInterfaceTooltip: Physical interface for outbound traffic. Use 'auto' to detect; auto-enabled when Auto system routes is set.
form.mtgDomainFrontingHint: Where mtg sends non-Telegram traffic — e.g. your NGINX fake site. Leave the IP empty to use the FakeTLS domain via DNS; default port is 443.
form.mtgRouteThroughXrayHint: Send this proxy's Telegram traffic through Xray so it follows your routing rules. The mtg sidecar dials out via a loopback SOCKS bridge tagged with this inbound's tag; reference that tag in the Routing tab for advanced rules.
form.mtgRouteOutboundHint: Optional. Force Telegram traffic out through this outbound (or balancer). Leave empty to let your routing rules decide.
form.mtprotoFakeTlsDomainHint: Default FakeTLS domain used to generate a new client's secret. Each client can front its own domain.
form.mtgThrottleMaxConnectionsHint: Cap concurrent connections across all users with a fair-share limit. 0 disables throttling.
form.mtgAdTagInvalid: Ad-tag must be exactly 32 hexadecimal characters.
form.mtgPublicIpHint: This server's reachable public address, used by the ad-tag middle proxy. Leave blank to let mtg auto-detect it.
form.sessionIDTableHint: Charset for generated session IDs: a predefined name (ALPHABET, Base62, hex, number, …) or a literal ASCII string. Leave empty for xray-core's default.
form.sessionIDLengthHint: Length or range (e.g. 8-16) of generated session IDs. Only used when a Session ID Table is set; minimum must be greater than 0.
form.tcpWindowClampHint: Leave 0 to use the OS default. Non-zero values cap the advertised TCP receive window; values like 600 (from the Xray docs example) can collapse throughput on high-latency links.
form.trustedXForwardedForHint: Trust this request header for the real client IP (e.g. CF-Connecting-IP behind Cloudflare's CDN). Only honored on WebSocket, HTTPUpgrade, XHTTP and gRPC transports. Leave empty to ignore forwarded headers.
form.proxyProtocolHint: Accept the PROXY-protocol header to learn the real client IP from an upstream L4 tunnel or relay (HAProxy, gost, nginx-stream, Xray dokodemo-door) or Cloudflare Spectrum. The upstream MUST emit PROXY protocol. Works on TCP, WebSocket, HTTPUpgrade and gRPC; not on mKCP.
form.realClientIpHint: Capture the visitor's real IP when traffic reaches this inbound through a CDN or relay, instead of recording the intermediary's address. Pick a preset to fill the matching sockopt fields below. These fields are never sent to clients in subscriptions.
form.realClientIpTrustedHeaderTransportWarn: Trusted X-Forwarded-For is only honored on WebSocket, HTTPUpgrade and XHTTP. On the current transport this header is ignored.
form.realClientIpProxyProtocolTransportWarn: PROXY protocol is not supported on this transport (mKCP). Use TCP/RAW, WebSocket, HTTPUpgrade, gRPC or XHTTP.
form.echSockoptTip: Socket options for the connection Xray uses to fetch the ECH config list (e.g. route the lookup through a dialerProxy outbound). Leave disabled to use defaults.
form.curvePreferencesTip: Restrict the TLS key-exchange curves the server offers, in preference order (e.g. X25519MLKEM768, X25519). Leave empty to use Xray-core defaults.
form.masterKeyLogTip: Path to write TLS master keys (SSLKEYLOGFILE format) for debugging with Wireshark. Leave empty in production — it lets anyone with the file decrypt traffic.
form.verifyPeerCertByNameTip: Tell clients to verify the server certificate against this name instead of the SNI. Comma-separated names. Panel-only — included in share links (vcn). The modern replacement for allowInsecure, which Xray removed after 2026-06-01.
form.pinnedPeerCertSha256Tip: SHA-256 hash(es) of the peer certificate as a hex string (e.g. e8e2d3…), comma-separated. Panel-only — not written to the server's xray config, but included in share links so clients can pin the certificate.
form.pinFromRemote: Fetch the hash by pinging the SNI (xray tls ping)
form.pinFromRemoteNoSni: Set the SNI (serverName) first to ping the remote certificate.
form.pinFromRemoteFailed: Could not fetch the remote certificate hash.
form.realityTargetHint: Required. Must include a port (e.g. example.com:443). Without a port Xray-core refuses to start.
form.realityTargetNeedsPort: REALITY target must include a port (e.g. example.com:443)
form.realityTargetInvalidPort: REALITY target has an invalid port
form.scanModalDesc: Validate a domain, or scan an IP / CIDR range to discover new REALITY targets from their certificates. Leave the box empty to probe common candidates.
form.scanDiscoverPlaceholder: IP, CIDR, or domain — leave empty for common candidates
form.spiderXHint: Per-client seed — the panel derives a unique spx path for each client from it; regenerate to rotate everyone's paths
form.afterBytesTip: Let the fallback run at full speed for this many bytes, then start throttling. 0 = throttle from the first byte.
form.bytesPerSecTip: Speed cap (bytes/sec) applied to fallback traffic after the threshold, so probes can't use your server as free bandwidth to the target. 0 = no limit (disables this direction).
form.burstBytesPerSecTip: Allowance for short bursts above the steady rate (token-bucket size). If lower than Bytes Per Sec it is raised to match.
form.listenHelp: You can also enter a Unix socket path (e.g. /run/xray/in.sock), or an abstract socket name prefixed with @ (e.g. @xray/in.sock), to listen on a socket instead of a TCP port — set Port to 0 in that case.
form.shareAddrStrategyHelp: Controls which address is written into exported share links, QR codes, and subscription output.
form.shareAddrHelp: Used only when the share address strategy is Custom. Enter a host or IP without a scheme or port.
form.subSortIndexHelp: Position of this inbound's links in subscription output (sub page and client apps). Lower values come first; equal values keep creation order. Does not affect the panel inbound list.

clients.linksHint: Add third-party share links and remote subscription URLs to include in this client's subscription.
clients.copySelectSourceFirst: Please select a source inbound first.
clients.copyResultNone: Nothing to copy: no clients selected or source is empty
clients.copyFlowHint: Applied to all copied clients. Leave empty to skip.
clients.searchPlaceholder: Search email, comment, sub ID, UUID, password, auth, Telegram ID…
clients.title: Clients
clients.totalGBDesc: Data quota for this client. 0 = unlimited.
clients.limitIpFail2banMissing: Fail2ban is not installed, so the IP limit cannot be enforced. Install Fail2ban from the x-ui bash menu to enable this option.
clients.limitIpFail2banWindows: Fail2ban is not available on Windows, so the IP limit cannot be enforced.
clients.limitIpDisabled: The IP limit feature is disabled on this server.
clients.passwordDesc: Only used by Trojan and Shadowsocks clients; ignored for VLESS, VMess, Hysteria, and WireGuard.
clients.emailInvalidChars: Email cannot contain spaces, '/', '\', or control characters
clients.subIdInvalidChars: Subscription ID cannot contain spaces, '/', '\', or control characters
clients.groupDesc: Logical label used to bucket related clients (e.g. team, customer, region). Filterable from the toolbar.
clients.noSubId: This client has no subId, no shareable link.
clients.noLinks: No shareable links — attach this client to a protocol-capable inbound first.
clients.resetNotPossible: Attach this client to an inbound first.
clients.resetAllTrafficsContent: Every client's up/down counter drops to zero. Quotas and expiry are not affected. This cannot be undone.
clients.deleteConfirmContent: This removes the client from every attached inbound and drops its traffic record. This cannot be undone.
clients.addToGroupTooltip: Pick an existing group or type a new name. Use the Ungroup action to remove clients from their current group.
clients.ungroupConfirmContent: Clears the group label on each selected client. Clients themselves are kept (use Delete to remove them entirely).
clients.bulkEnableConfirmContent: Enables each selected client on every attached inbound. Clients whose quota is exhausted or whose expiry has passed will be disabled again automatically.
clients.bulkDisableConfirmContent: Disables each selected client on every attached inbound. They lose access immediately but their records and traffic are kept.
clients.attachToInboundsDesc: Attaches the selected {count} client(s) (same UUID/password and shared traffic) to the chosen inbound(s). They keep their existing attachments too.
clients.attachToInboundsNoTargets: No multi-user inbounds available to attach to.
clients.detachFromInboundsDesc: Removes the selected {count} client(s) from the chosen inbound(s). Pairs where the client wasn't attached are silently skipped. Client records are kept (use Delete to remove fully).
clients.subLinksEmpty: None of the selected clients have a subscription ID.
clients.subLinksDisabledHint: Enable subscription in Panel Settings → Subscription to generate links.
clients.bulkDeleteConfirmContent: Each selected client is removed from every attached inbound and its traffic record is dropped. This cannot be undone.
clients.bulkAdjustHint: Positive values extend, negative values reduce. Clients with unlimited expiry or traffic are skipped for that field.
clients.bulkAdjustNothing: Set days, traffic, or flow before applying.
clients.delDepletedConfirmContent: Removes every client whose traffic quota is exhausted or whose expiry has passed. This cannot be undone.
clients.delOrphansConfirmContent: Removes every client that is not attached to any inbound, along with its traffic record. This cannot be undone.
clients.hysteriaAuthDesc: Credential used only by Hysteria clients. Trojan and Shadowsocks use the Password field instead.
clients.wireguardAllowedIPsHint: Leave empty to auto-assign; separate entries with commas
clients.mtprotoSecretHint: The client's FakeTLS secret. Regenerate to rotate it.
clients.mtprotoAdTagHint: Optional 32-character hex tag from Telegram's proxy registration. When set, this client is routed through Telegram middle proxies and a sponsored channel appears at the top of their chat list.

groups.title: Groups
groups.name: Name
groups.deleteConfirmContent: This removes the group and clears its label from {count} client(s). The clients themselves are not deleted.
groups.resetConfirmContent: This resets only the group's traffic counter. Individual client counters are not affected.
groups.emptyForAction: This group has no clients yet.
groups.deleteClientsConfirmContent: This permanently removes {count} client(s) along with their traffic records. The group label is cleared too. This cannot be undone.
groups.addToGroupDesc: Select clients to add to this group. They keep their existing inbound attachments; only the group label changes. Clients already in this group are not listed.
groups.addToGroupEmpty: No other clients available to add.
groups.removeFromGroupDesc: Select members to remove from this group. Clients themselves are kept (use "Delete clients in group" to remove them entirely).

hints.address: Leave blank to inherit the inbound's own address.
hints.tags: Not visible to end users; sent with RAW subscription only. Uppercase letters, digits, _ and : only.
hints.nodeGuids: Pick nodes which resolved from this host. Only visual assignment.
hints.serverDescription: Optional note shown under the remark.
hints.vlessRoute: Single VLESS route value (0-65535) baked into the UUID, e.g. 443. Leave blank for none.
hints.remark: A plain label for this host. Shown as the config name only when the inbound has no remark of its own.

remarkVars.title: Template Variables
remarkVars.intro: Click a variable to add it. It is replaced per client when the subscription is generated.
remarkVars.descUSAGE_PERCENTAGE: Used traffic as a percentage (hidden if unlimited)
remarkVars.descJALALI_EXPIRE_DATE: Expiry date in the Jalali calendar (YYYY/MM/DD)
remarkVars.descCREATED_UNIX: Creation time as a Unix timestamp (seconds)

toasts.badVlessRoute: Enter a single number between 0 and 65535

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.name: Name
nodes.apiTokenPlaceholder: Token from the remote panel's Settings page
nodes.apiTokenHint: The remote panel exposes its API token under Authentication → API Token.
nodes.regenerateConfirm: Regenerating invalidates the current token. Any central panel using it will lose access until updated. Continue?
nodes.allowPrivateAddressHint: Enable only for nodes on a private network or VPN.
nodes.outboundTagHint: Route this node's panel API traffic through the selected Xray outbound. A loopback bridge inbound is added to the running config automatically and applied live. Leave empty for a direct connection.
nodes.inboundSyncModeHint: Choose which inbounds are imported from this node. Existing nodes default to all inbounds.
nodes.inboundTagsHint: Selection is matched by the inbound tag. An empty selection imports none.
nodes.updateConfirmTitle: Update {count} node(s) to the latest version?
nodes.updateConfirmContent: Each selected node downloads the latest release and restarts onto it. Only enabled, online nodes are updated.
nodes.subNodeTip: Read-only: a downstream node reached through {parent}. Manage it from {parent}'s own panel.
nodes.deleteConfirmContent: This stops monitoring the node. The remote panel itself is unaffected.

toasts.fillRequired: Name, address, port and API token are required
toasts.updateNoneEligible: Select at least one online, enabled node

nodes.tlsVerifyModeHint: How the panel validates the node's HTTPS certificate. Pin or Skip are for self-signed certs (https nodes only).
nodes.mtlsFormHint: This node authenticates the panel with a client certificate. Copy this panel's CA from the Node mTLS section onto the node, set its Trusted parent CA, then restart it.

mtls.title: Node mTLS
mtls.intro: Mutual TLS adds a client-certificate factor on top of the API token for node-to-node calls. It is opt-in: leave it empty to keep token-only auth.
mtls.copyCaHint: Hand this CA to the nodes this panel manages, then set their TLS verification to Mutual TLS.
mtls.caFailed: Failed to obtain the CA certificate
mtls.trustHint: When this panel is itself a node, paste the managing panel's CA here to require its client certificate. Restart the panel to apply.
mtls.saved: Trust CA saved — restart the panel to apply

nodes.tlsSkipWarning: Skipping verification removes protection against man-in-the-middle attacks — the API token could be intercepted. Prefer pinning the certificate.
nodes.pinnedCertHint: Base64 or hex SHA-256 of the node's certificate. Use Fetch to read it from the node now.

settings.title: Panel Settings
settings.infoDesc: Every change made here needs to be saved. Please restart the panel to apply changes.
settings.restartPanelDesc: Are you sure you want to restart the panel? If you cannot access the panel after restarting, please view the panel log info on the server.
settings.warnHttp: Panel is served over plain HTTP — set up TLS for production.
settings.warnDefaultPort: Default port 2053 is well-known — change it to a random port.
settings.warnDefaultBasePath: Default base path "/" is well-known — change it to a random path.
settings.warnDefaultSubPath: Default subscription path "/sub/" is well-known — change it.
settings.warnDefaultJsonPath: Default JSON subscription path "/json/" is well-known — change it.
settings.panelListeningIPDesc: The IP address for the web panel. (leave blank to listen on all IPs)
settings.panelListeningDomainDesc: The domain name for the web panel. (leave blank to listen on all domains and IPs)
settings.panelPortDesc: The port number for the web panel. (must be an unused port)
settings.publicKeyPathDesc: The public key file path for the web panel. (begins with ‘/‘)
settings.privateKeyPathDesc: The private key file path for the web panel. (begins with ‘/‘)
settings.panelUrlPathDesc: The URI path for the web panel. (begins with ‘/‘ and concludes with ‘/‘)
settings.pageSizeDesc: Define page size for inbounds table. (0 = disable)
settings.panelOutboundDesc: Routes the panel's own requests — panel/Xray version checks and downloads, Telegram, and the normal geo-file update — through this Xray outbound to bypass server-side filtering of GitHub/Telegram. A loopback bridge inbound is added to the running config automatically and applied live. The Xray-native Geodata Auto-Update is not affected; it has its own download outbound. Leave empty for a direct connection.
settings.remarkTemplateDesc: When set, this replaces the remark model for every subscription link — write your own format with the variable tokens (use the button to insert them). Leave empty to use the model above.
settings.datepickerDescription: Scheduled tasks will run based on this calendar.
settings.telegramTokenDesc: The Telegram bot token obtained from '{'@'}BotFather'.
settings.telegramProxyDesc: Enables SOCKS5 proxy for connecting to Telegram. (adjust settings as per guide)
settings.telegramAPIServerDesc: The Telegram API server to use. Leave blank to use the default server.
settings.telegramChatIdDesc: The Telegram Admin Chat ID(s). (comma-separated)(get it here {'@'}userinfobot) or (use '/id' command in the bot)
settings.telegramNotifyTimeDesc: How often the Telegram bot sends periodic reports. Pick a preset interval, or choose Custom to enter a raw crontab expression.
settings.tgNotifyBackupDesc: Send a database backup file with a report.
settings.tgNotifyLoginDesc: Get notified about the username, IP address, and time whenever someone attempts to log into your web panel.
settings.sessionMaxAgeDesc: The duration for which you can stay logged in. (unit: minute)
settings.expireTimeDiffDesc: Get notified about expiration date when reaching this threshold. (unit: day)
settings.trafficDiffDesc: Get notified about traffic cap when reaching this threshold. (unit: GB)
settings.tgNotifyCpuDesc: Get notified if CPU load exceeds this threshold. (unit: %)
settings.timeZoneDesc: Scheduled tasks will run based on this time zone.
settings.subJsonEnable: Enable/Disable the JSON subscription endpoint independently.
settings.subTitle: Subscription Title
settings.subSupportUrlDesc: Technical support link shown in the VPN client
settings.subProfileUrlDesc: A link to your website displayed in the VPN client
settings.subAnnounceDesc: The announcement text displayed in the VPN client
settings.subThemeDirDesc: Absolute path to a folder containing a custom index.html/sub.html subscription page template (e.g. /etc/3x-ui/sub_templates/my-theme/). Leave empty to use the default page.
settings.subEnableRoutingDesc: Global setting to enable routing in the VPN client. (Only for Happ)
settings.subRoutingRulesDesc: Global routing rules for the VPN client. (Only for Happ)
settings.subHideSettingsDesc: Hide the ability to view and edit server configurations in the VPN client. (Only for Happ)
settings.subIncyEnableRoutingDesc: Inject a routing profile into the subscription body for the Incy client. (Only for Incy)
settings.subIncyRoutingRulesDesc: Incy routing deep-link added to the subscription body, e.g. incy://routing/onadd/<base64>. (Only for Incy)
settings.subClashEnableRoutingDesc: Include global Clash/Mihomo routing rules in generated YAML subscriptions.
settings.subClashRoutingRulesDesc: Default Clash/Mihomo rules prepended to every generated YAML subscription before MATCH,PROXY.
settings.subListenDesc: The IP address for the subscription service. (leave blank to listen on all IPs)
settings.subPortDesc: The port number for the subscription service. (must be an unused port). Also used to build the subscription link/QR shown in the panel when "Reverse Proxy URI" below is empty — if the subscription is reached through a reverse proxy on a different port, set "Reverse Proxy URI" instead.
settings.subCertPathDesc: The public key file path for the subscription service. (begins with ‘/‘)
settings.subKeyPathDesc: The private key file path for the subscription service. (begins with ‘/‘)
settings.subPathDesc: The URI path for the subscription service. (begins with ‘/‘ and concludes with ‘/‘)
settings.subDomainDesc: The domain name for the subscription service. (leave blank to listen on all domains and IPs). Also used as the fallback domain for the displayed subscription link when "Reverse Proxy URI" is empty — set "Reverse Proxy URI" if the panel and the subscription are reached through different domains (e.g. behind a reverse proxy).
settings.subUpdatesDesc: The update intervals of the subscription URL in the client apps. (unit: hour)
settings.subEncryptDesc: The returned content of subscription service will be Base64 encoded.
settings.subURIDesc: The full base URL (scheme://domain[:port]/path/) for the subscription link and QR code, used instead of Listen Domain/Listen Port. Set this whenever the subscription is reached through a reverse proxy or a domain/port different from the ones above.
settings.externalTrafficInformEnableDesc: Inform external API on every traffic update.
settings.externalTrafficInformURIDesc: Traffic updates are sent to this URI.
settings.restartXrayOnClientDisableDesc: When a client is automatically disabled due to expiration or traffic limit, restart Xray.
settings.fragmentDesc: Enable fragmentation for TLS hello packet.
settings.trustedProxyCidrsDesc: Comma-separated IPs/CIDRs allowed to set forwarded host, proto, and client IP headers.

ldap.skipTlsVerifyDesc: Insecure — disables server certificate validation. Use only with internal/untrusted CAs.
ldap.passwordConfigured: Configured; leave blank to keep current password.
ldap.passwordPlaceholder: Configured - enter a new value to replace
ldap.flagFieldDesc: If set, overrides VLESS flag — e.g. shadowInactive.
ldap.invertFlagDesc: Enable when the attribute means disabled (e.g. shadowInactive).
ldap.inboundTagsDesc: Inbounds that LDAP sync may auto-create or auto-delete clients on.
ldap.noInbounds: No inbounds found. Create one in Inbounds first.

subFormats.finalMaskDesc: xray finalmask masks (TCP/UDP) and QUIC tuning injected into every JSON subscription stream. Requires a recent xray client.

settings.muxDesc: Transmit multiple independent data streams within an established data stream.
settings.directDesc: Directly establishes connections with domains or IP ranges of a specific country.

security.twoFactorEnableDesc: Adds an additional layer of authentication to provide more security.
security.twoFactorModalSteps: To set up two-factor authentication, perform a few steps:
security.twoFactorModalFirstStep: 1. Scan this QR code in the app for authentication or copy the token near the QR code and paste it into the app
security.twoFactorModalSecondStep: 2. Enter the code from the app
security.twoFactorModalRemoveStep: Enter the code from the application to remove two-factor authentication.
security.twoFactorModalChangeCredentialsStep: Enter the code from the application to change administrator credentials.
security.twoFactorModalSetSuccess: Two-factor authentication has been successfully established
security.twoFactorModalDeleteSuccess: Two-factor authentication has been successfully deleted
security.apiTokenEmpty: No tokens yet — create one to authenticate bots or remote panels.
security.apiTokenDeleteWarning: Any caller using this token will stop authenticating immediately.
security.apiTokenCreatedNotice: Copy this token now. For security it is not stored in readable form and will not be shown again.

toasts.getSettings: An error occurred while retrieving parameters.
toasts.modifyUserError: An error occurred while changing administrator credentials.
toasts.modifyUser: You have successfully changed the credentials of the administrator.
toasts.originalUserPassIncorrect: The current username or password is invalid
toasts.userPassMustBeNotEmpty: The new username and password are empty

settings.smtpEventBusNotifyDesc: Select which events trigger email notifications
settings.tgEventBusNotifyDesc: Select which events trigger Telegram notifications
settings.telegramTokenConfigured: Configured; leave blank to keep current token.
settings.telegramTokenPlaceholder: Configured - enter a new token to replace
settings.smtpPasswordConfigured: Configured; leave blank to keep current password.
settings.smtpPasswordPlaceholder: Configured - enter a new password to replace
settings.smtpErrorAuth: Authentication failed — check username and password
settings.smtpErrorStarttls: Server requires STARTTLS — change encryption type
settings.smtpErrorTls: Server requires TLS — change encryption type
settings.smtpErrorRefused: Connection refused — check host and port
settings.smtpErrorRelay: Server rejects sending from this address

xray.title: Xray Configs
xray.restartConfirmContent: Reloads the xray service with the saved configuration.
xray.restartError: There was an error when rebooting the Xray.
xray.stopError: There was an error when stopping the Xray.
xray.generalConfigsDesc: These options will determine general adjustments.
xray.logConfigsDesc: Logs may affect your server's efficiency. It is recommended to enable them wisely only when needed.
xray.blockConfigsDesc: These options will block traffic based on specific requested protocols and websites.
xray.blockConnectionsConfigsDesc: These options will block traffic based on the specific requested country.
xray.directConnectionsConfigsDesc: A direct connection ensures that specific traffic is not routed through another server.
xray.ipv4RoutingDesc: These options will route traffic based on a specific destination via IPv4.
xray.warpRoutingDesc: These options will route traffic based on a specific destination via WARP.
xray.nordRoutingDesc: These options will route traffic based on a specific destination via NordVPN.
xray.TemplateDesc: The final Xray config file will be generated based on this template.
xray.FreedomStrategyDesc: Set the output strategy for the network in the Freedom Protocol.
xray.FreedomHappyEyeballsDesc: Dual-stack dialing for the direct (freedom) outbound — useful on exit servers with both IPv4 and IPv6.
xray.FreedomHappyEyeballsTryDelayDesc: Milliseconds before trying the alternate address family. 150–250 ms is a good starting point.
xray.RoutingStrategyDesc: Set the overall traffic routing strategy for resolving all requests.
xray.outboundTestUrlDesc: URL used when testing outbound connectivity.
xray.OutboundSubscriptionsDesc: Import outbounds from remote subscription URLs (vmess/vless/trojan/ss/...). Tags are kept stable for use in balancers and routing rules. Updates are automatic.
xray.balancerNotRunning: This balancer is not active in the running Xray — save your changes or start Xray first
xray.routeTesterDesc: Ask the running Xray which outbound would handle a connection. No traffic is sent — the decision comes straight from the live routing engine.
xray.routeTesterDefaultOutbound: No routing rule matched — traffic goes to the default (first) outbound.
xray.RoutingsDesc: The priority of each rule is important!
xray.importInvalidJson: Invalid JSON — expected an array or an object with a matching key.
xray.logLevelDesc: The log level for error logs, indicating the information that needs to be recorded.
xray.accessLogDesc: The file path for the access log. The special value 'none' disables access logs
xray.errorLogDesc: The file path for the error log. The special value 'none' disables error logs
xray.dnsLogDesc: Whether to enable DNS query logs
xray.maskAddressDesc: IP address mask, when enabled, will automatically replace the IP address that appears in the log.
xray.statsInboundUplinkDesc: Enables the statistics collection for upstream traffic of all inbound proxies.
xray.statsInboundDownlinkDesc: Enables the statistics collection for downstream traffic of all inbound proxies.
xray.statsOutboundUplinkDesc: Enables the statistics collection for upstream traffic of all outbound proxies.
xray.statsOutboundDownlinkDesc: Enables the statistics collection for downstream traffic of all outbound proxies.
xray.metricsListenDesc: Expose Xray's Prometheus-style metrics on this address:port (e.g. 127.0.0.1:11111). Leave empty to disable. Bind to localhost and reverse-proxy it — it is unauthenticated.
xray.connectionLimitsDesc: Connection-level policies for user level 0. Leave a field empty to use Xray's default.
xray.connIdleDesc: Closes a connection after it stays idle for this many seconds. Lowering it frees memory and file descriptors faster on busy servers (Xray default: 300).
xray.bufferSizeDesc: Per-connection internal buffer size in KB. Set to 0 to minimize memory usage on low-RAM servers (Xray default depends on the platform).

ruleForm.balancerTagTooltip: Routes traffic through one of the configured load balancers

outboundForm.tagDuplicate: Tag already used by another outbound
outboundForm.dialerProxyPlaceholder: Select an outbound to chain through
outboundForm.dialerProxyHint: Dial this outbound through another outbound (by tag) to build a proxy chain. Leave empty to connect directly.
outboundForm.targetStrategyHint: How the destination domain is resolved before connecting: AsIs (default) sends it unresolved, UseIP… resolves with fallback, ForceIP… requires successful resolution.
outboundForm.inboundTagPlaceholder: inbound tag used in routing rules

outbound.reverseTagDesc: VLESS simple reverse proxy tag. Leave empty to disable.
outbound.testModeTooltip: TCP: fast dial-only probe. HTTP: full request through xray. Real delay: total time including connection setup.

outboundSub.title: Outbound Subscriptions
outboundSub.intervalHint: Default 10 minutes. The background job checks frequently; each subscription only re-fetches when its own interval has passed.
outboundSub.allowPrivateHint: Permit localhost / LAN / private IPs for this subscription's URL. Off by default for security — enable only for a trusted local source.
outboundSub.prependHint: Place this subscription's outbounds before your manual ones, so one can become the default.
outboundSub.previewEmpty: No outbounds found at this URL.
outboundSub.empty: No subscriptions yet. Add one above.
outboundSub.restartHint: After adding or refreshing, restart Xray (or wait for the next auto-reload) to make the outbounds active.
outboundSub.fromSubsDesc: Imported from your active subscriptions. Manage them in the Subscriptions panel above.

observatory.title: Observatory
observatory.autoManaged: Observers are managed automatically from your balancers. Tune how they probe below — the watched outbounds follow your balancer selectors.
observatory.emptyHint: No connection observer is active. One is added automatically when you create a Least Ping or Least Load balancer — or a Random / Round-robin balancer with a fallback — so observer-backed balancers can check outbound health before choosing a target.
observatory.mixedLegacy: This config contains both Observatory and Burst Observatory. Xray uses one global observer, so this mixed legacy state is not supported; saving balancers will normalize it to one observer.
observatory.subjectSelectorDesc: Outbound tags this observer probes. Managed automatically from your balancers.
observatory.probeURLDesc: URL fetched to measure each outbound. Should return HTTP 204.
observatory.probeIntervalDesc: How often to probe each outbound, e.g. 30s, 1m, 2h45m.
observatory.enableConcurrencyDesc: Probe all watched outbounds at once instead of one-by-one. Faster, but more visible on the network.
observatory.destinationDesc: URL fetched to measure each outbound. Should return HTTP 204.
observatory.connectivityDesc: Optional local-network check URL, tried only after the destination fails. Leave empty to skip.
observatory.intervalDesc: Average time between probes per outbound, e.g. 1m. Minimum 10s.
observatory.timeoutDesc: How long to wait for a probe before it counts as failed, e.g. 5s.
observatory.samplingDesc: Number of recent probe results kept to score each outbound.
observatory.deleteAlsoObservatory: This is the last balancer using the Observatory, so it will be removed too.
observatory.deleteAlsoBurst: This is the last balancer using the Burst Observatory, so it will be removed too.

refCleanup.header: Deleting this also updates your routing:

balancer.tagDuplicate: Tag already used by another balancer
balancer.balancerFallbackInfo: Traffic will be routed through: Balancer → Loopback → Server → Target Balancer → Outbound. This adds an extra hop through the server, which may introduce slight delays.
balancer.balancerFallbackCycle: Cannot set this balancer as fallback — it would create a circular dependency.
balancer.balancerDeleteInUse: Cannot delete this balancer — it is used as fallback by: {names}
balancer.reservedPrefix: _bl_ prefix is reserved for internal balancer loopback objects
balancer.balancerDesc: It is not possible to use balancerTag and outboundTag at the same time. If used at the same time, only outboundTag will work.

tun.nameDesc: The name of the TUN interface. Default is 'xray0'
tun.mtuDesc: Maximum Transmission Unit. The maximum size of data packets. Default is 1500
tun.userLevelDesc: All connections made through this inbound will use this user level. Default is 0

nord.noServers: No servers found for the selected country
nord.noPublicKey: Selected server does not advertise a NordLynx public key.

warp.intervalDesc: 0 to disable. Changes IP address automatically.

dns.tagDesc: This tag will be available as an Inbound tag in routing rules.
dns.clientIpDesc: Used to notify the server of the specified IP location during DNS queries
dns.disableFallbackIfMatchDesc: Disables fallback DNS queries when the matching domain list of the DNS server is hit
dns.enableParallelQueryDesc: Enable parallel DNS queries to multiple servers for faster resolution
dns.strategyDesc: Overall strategy to resolve domain names
dns.useSystemHostsDesc: Use the operating system's hosts file
dns.serveStaleDesc: Return expired cached results while refreshing in the background
dns.serveExpiredTTLDesc: Validity (seconds) of stale cache entries; 0 = never expire
dns.hostsValues: IP or domain — type and press Enter
dns.clearAllConfirm: This removes every DNS server from the list. This cannot be undone.
dns.dnsLeakWarning: DNS can leak through localhost, plain UDP/TCP, local-mode DoH/DoQ, fallback queries, or EDNS client IP. Use routed DoH, hosts pins, and disable fallback when privacy matters.

xray.defaultOutboundDesc: Traffic that does not match any routing rule uses this outbound (Xray uses the first outbound in the list).

tgbot.noQuery: ❌ Query not found! Please use the command again!

commands.help: 🤖 Welcome to this bot! It's designed to offer specific data from the web panel and allows you to make modifications as needed.
commands.usage: ❗ Please provide a text to search!
commands.helpAdminCommands: To restart Xray Core: /restart To search for a client email: /usage [Email] To search for inbounds (with client stats): /inbound [Remark] Telegram Chat ID: /id
commands.helpClientCommands: To search for statistics, use the following command: /usage [Email] Telegram Chat ID: /id

messages.loginSuccess: ✅ Logged in to the panel successfully.
messages.cancel:

You can /start again anytime. 🔄
messages.using_default_value: Okay, I'll stick with the default value. 😊
messages.incorrect_input:
Your input is not valid.
The phrases should be continuous without spaces.
Correct example: aaaaaa
Incorrect example: aaa aaa 🚫
messages.FinishProcess: 🔚 Traffic reset process finished for all clients.
messages.memoryThreshold: Memory Load {{ .Percent }}% exceeds the threshold of {{ .Threshold }}%

answers.askToAddUserId: Your configuration is not found! Please ask your admin to use your Telegram ChatID in your configuration(s). Your ChatID: {{ .TgUserID }}
